error.signature: google.auth.exceptions.RefreshError: ('invalid_grant: Token has been expired or revoked.',)

verdict.summary: Google silently revokes refresh tokens after 6 months of inactivity, when user changes password, or when >50 tokens exist per client. The 50-token limit is barely documented.

dead_ends.action: Store and reuse the refresh token indefinitely
dead_ends.why_fails: Google has a hidden 50 refresh token limit per user per OAuth client. Token 51 silently invalidates token 1. Also, 6 months inactivity = revocation.

dead_ends.action: Request a new refresh token on every auth flow
dead_ends.why_fails: Each new token pushes out the oldest. If user has multiple devices, they kick each other out.

workarounds.action: Store refresh tokens per-user and reuse existing ones via prompt=none
workarounds.how: If user already has a refresh token in your DB, use it instead of initiating new OAuth flow

workarounds.action: Implement re-authentication flow that gracefully handles revocation
Use service accounts for server-to-server auth instead of user OAuth
